title: Enterprise Account Executive (Founding)
companyindustry: Software Development and Technology, Information and Media
descriptionhtml:
Enterprise Account Executive

Sauced. EMEA (Remote)

Save


Apply


Our Client

Founded in 2019, our client is a fast-growing AI infrastructure business with offices in San Francisco and an expanding EMEA presence. With ~55 employees globally, they've built one of the world's most widely used open source data labelling platforms — trusted by over 350,000 data scientists and ML engineers worldwide.


Their platform enables enterprises to train, evaluate, and fine-tune AI models using high-quality annotated data, combining powerful software with managed data services for large organisations building AI at scale. They work with some of the world's most sophisticated teams investing heavily in machine learning and AI development.


Grown from $400k ARR in 2021 to ~$10M today and projected to double this year, they've achieved this without relying on heavy external investment — a rare thing in this space. As demand for AI and machine learning infrastructure continues to accelerate, they are scaling quickly and the European market represents a major growth opportunity.


What You'll Get

- £80,000–£120,000 base salary (depending on experience)

- Double OTE (Uncapped)

- Equity

- Remote-first (Western Europe) OR a couple of days per week in the London office (only if you're based there)


You'll be joining a business selling a highly technical and increasingly mission-critical platform into enterprise organisations at the forefront of AI adoption — with real scope to grow as the European business scales.


What You'll Do

This is a key EMEA hire, working directly with the Head of EMEA and global sales leadership to build out the European business. It's a genuine ground-floor opportunity to shape how the company goes to market across the region.


You'll run full-cycle enterprise sales processes, selling into technical and senior stakeholders — data scientists, ML engineers, Heads of AI, CTOs, and VP-level buyers. Deal sizes typically range from ~$100k to $120k+, with larger enterprise opportunities up to ~$600k, and sales cycles of around five months involving multiple stakeholders across the business.

You'll be expected to generate a portion of your own pipeline alongside strong inbound demand generated through their large and active open-source community — a genuine differentiator in terms of brand awareness and warm engagement.


What You'll Need

- A proven track record in complex, full-cycle enterprise SaaS sales with annual quotas of $1M+

- Confidence selling technical products and navigating multi-stakeholder sales processes

- Experience selling into engineering, data, or AI teams is highly desirable

- A background in AI, data, developer tools, or technical SaaS would be a strong advantage

- Comfortable in a scale-up environment — Open to coaching, proactive and have the right attitude: sleeves up, hands dirty, self-starter!

title: Enterprise Account Executive | Remote UK | CRM & CX | Martech | SaaS | AI | Unicorn |
jobfunction: Sales, General Business, and Business Development
companyindustry: Software Development and IT System Custom Software Development
descriptionhtml:
Enterprise Account Executive

📍 UK / Ireland (Remote)

💰 £140k–£220k OTE (50/50 split)


There are not many opportunities where you can join a company right after Unicorn status, with €200M+ ARR, insane product-market fit, massive funding behind them, and still get the chance to help build a market almost from scratch.


This is one of them.


A high-growth SaaS company in the CRM / customer engagement space is scaling its UK&I enterprise team after a major funding round and huge global growth. Their platform sits across email, SMS, WhatsApp, chat and marketing automation — helping businesses manage customer engagement at scale through one unified platform.


The business already has serious global traction, but the UK enterprise team is still relatively small, making this a rare opportunity to combine the backing and stability of a scaled company with the ownership and excitement of an early-stage build.


🏢 About the company

  • Global SaaS platform in CRM / CX / Marketing Automation
  • €200M+ ARR with extremely strong product-market fit
  • Recently became a Unicorn following a major funding round
  • 500,000+ customers globally
  • Huge investment into AI and future product innovation
  • Small but growing UK enterprise team
  • Strong internal mobility and progression opportunities


🏋️ About the role

  • Full-cycle Mid-Market & Enterprise sales role
  • Highly outbound and self-generated environment
  • Own complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles
  • Help shape the UK GTM motion as the team scales
  • High autonomy, high visibility, high ownership
  • Work closely with international leadership and cross-functional teams


🔍 What they’re looking for

  • 3–8+ years of B2B SaaS closing experience
  • MUST have CRM, CX, Marketing Automation or MarTech sales experience
  • Strong outbound / self-generated pipeline ability
  • Experience managing complex enterprise sales cycles
  • Consistent quota attainment
  • Someone commercially sharp, proactive and comfortable operating autonomously


This is the kind of role where the timing matters just as much as the company itself. Huge momentum, huge backing, and still early enough to have a real impact on how the UK market gets built.

title: Senior Account Executive, EMEA Enterprise
descriptionhtml:
About Outreach

Outreach, founded in 2014, is the only complete agentic AI platform for revenue teams. Outreach infuses agentic AI, conversation intelligence, and assistive AI to power hundreds of use cases across revenue motions. From new logo prospecting to expansions, deal acceleration, driving retention, and forecasting, Outreach AI automates workflows and frees sellers to focus on more strategic conversations and actions. Revenue leaders benefit from connected account visibility, performance insights, and higher forecasting accuracy across every GTM team. World leading enterprise organizations use Outreach to power their revenue teams, including Databricks, SAP, Siemens, and Verizon to name a few.

About The Team

Our Enterprise Account Executive team partners with our potential customers that have 5,000 - 10,000 employees. They are responsible for managing the full deal cycles from generating opportunities to closing prospective customers. We are product experts and ambassadors for Outreach. We build deep knowledge and understanding of our customers' goals and pain points to enable them to have the insights to Create & Close pipeline through the Outreach Sales Execution Platform.

We enjoy learning new sales trends & methodologies, going to industry events, subscribing to blogs/podcasts, reading books, and researching case studies so we can grow together. We work closely together to champion our customers, share success stories, celebrate our wins, and share goals.

The Role

The primary purpose of a Senior Account Executive at Outreach is to manage complex, full-life cycle sales strategy for new accounts. You are responsible for leading the entire sales cycle including researching, prospecting, advising, negotiating contracts, and closing deals. You are accountable for developing account plans, using the MEDDPICC sales methodology to lead to successful building of new business to achieve your territory goals. You are able to identify and deeply understand a prospect’s needs, pains, and desired business outcomes to build an effective business case that demonstrates ROI and value proposition of the Outreach platform. You will partner with other members of the go-to-market team, such as Solution Consultants, to successfully navigate proof of concept demos to prospective clients. Your primary points of contact at prospective new customers are above the line contacts (CRO, COO, CEO, CFO, and CISO). Key performance indicators include quota attainment, pipeline generation and coverage, and forecast quality and accuracy.

Location: London, UK. Hybrid, in office 2 days a week.

Your Daily Adventures

  • Identify, research, and qualify potential new customers in your assigned territory.
  • Build effective pipeline coverage to achieve your sales targets and goals.
  • Forecast deals appropriately and accurately using Outreach’s forecast methodology.
  • Develop and deliver on account plans, using MEDDPICC Sales Methodology, to strategically increase new revenue for Outreach.
  • Conduct effective discovery calls to identify and unlock business challenges that Outreach solves.
  • Demonstrate how the Outreach platform provides a tailored solution to a prospect’s key outcomes they are trying to achieve.
  • Partner effectively with internal resources to drive and close business, effectively navigating team members across go-to-market, as well as finance and leadership.
  • Negotiate contracts with cross-functional teams including C-suite, finance, procurement, and technical teams.
  • Understand prospect’s needs to develop accurate scoping and success criteria to position a successful implementation.
  • Provide smooth account transition once landed to professional services and account management teams for future adoption and expansion opportunities.
  • Operate with high integrity while adhering to internal processes and sales methodologies.
  • Perform other duties as assigned.

Basic Qualifications

  • At least eight years of sales lifecycle management experience, preferably in a SaaS environment
  • Proven experience in selling disruptive, complex solutions into medium to large organizations
  • Proven experience in selling into accounts through a top down executive motion
  • Ability to manage and navigate long sales cycles (12 months) with contract values up to $200k - $500k
  • Ability to build and cultivate strong, trusting relationships and partnerships, both internally and externally
  • Knowledge of the MEDDPICC Sales Methodology and experience applying and using this framework for successful sales
  • Strong pipeline management skills
  • Strong negotiation skills
  • Effective communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to tailor messaging appropriately for the audience
  • Executive presence and interpersonal skills
  • Strong business and financial acumen to be able to showcase metrics and potential ROI
  • Team player with a high sense of drive and initiative to keep opportunities moving forward to create a winning culture

Why You’ll Love It Here

  • 25 days holiday + 8 bank holidays
  • Outreach contributes with monthly contribution towards your pension
  • Private medical care for employee and spouse/family with Program Health Plus
  • Cashplan is offered through Medicash to help offset out of pocket medical related expenses
  • Dental coverage
  • Life insurance at 4x annual salary
  • 16 weeks of annual top up maternity leave pay or 8 weeks of fully paid paternity leave
  • A parental leave program that includes not just extended time off but options for a paid night nurse
  • Opportunity to be part of company success via equity program
  • Diversity and inclusion programs that promote employee resource groups like OWN+ (Outreach Women's Network), Adelante (Latinx community), OBX (Outreach Black Connection), Mosaic (AAPI community), Pride (LGBTQIA+), Gender+, Disability Community, and Veterans/Military
  • Employee referral bonuses to encourage the addition of great new people to the team

Outreach is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

Our success is reliant on building teams that include people from different backgrounds and experiences who can elevate assumptions and ideas with fresh perspectives. We're dedicated to hiring the whole human, not just a resume. To that end, we look for a diverse pool of applicants-including those from historically marginalized groups. We would like to invite you to apply even if you don't think you meet all of the requirements listed below. We don't want a few lines in a job description to get between us and the opportunity to meet you.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

description_html:
About Outreach

Outreach, founded in 2014, is the only complete agentic AI platform for revenue teams. Outreach infuses agentic AI, conversation intelligence, and assistive AI to power hundreds of use cases across revenue motions. From new logo prospecting to expansions, deal acceleration, driving retention, and forecasting, Outreach AI automates workflows and frees sellers to focus on more strategic conversations and actions. Revenue leaders benefit from connected account visibility, performance insights, and higher forecasting accuracy across every GTM team. World leading enterprise organizations use Outreach to power their revenue teams, including Databricks, SAP, Siemens, and Verizon to name a few.

About The Team

Our Enterprise Account Executive team partners with our potential customers that have 5,000 - 10,000 employees. They are responsible for managing the full deal cycles from generating opportunities to closing prospective customers. We are product experts and ambassadors for Outreach. We build deep knowledge and understanding of our customers' goals and pain points to enable them to have the insights to Create & Close pipeline through the Outreach Sales Execution Platform.

We enjoy learning new sales trends & methodologies, going to industry events, subscribing to blogs/podcasts, reading books, and researching case studies so we can grow together. We work closely together to champion our customers, share success stories, celebrate our wins, and share goals.

The Role

The primary purpose of a Senior Account Executive at Outreach is to manage complex, full-life cycle sales strategy for new accounts. You are responsible for leading the entire sales cycle including researching, prospecting, advising, negotiating contracts, and closing deals. You are accountable for developing account plans, using the MEDDPICC sales methodology to lead to successful building of new business to achieve your territory goals. You are able to identify and deeply understand a prospect’s needs, pains, and desired business outcomes to build an effective business case that demonstrates ROI and value proposition of the Outreach platform. You will partner with other members of the go-to-market team, such as Solution Consultants, to successfully navigate proof of concept demos to prospective clients. Your primary points of contact at prospective new customers are above the line contacts (CRO, COO, CEO, CFO, and CISO). Key performance indicators include quota attainment, pipeline generation and coverage, and forecast quality and accuracy.

Location: London, UK. Hybrid, in office 2 days a week.

Your Daily Adventures

  • Identify, research, and qualify potential new customers in your assigned territory.
  • Build effective pipeline coverage to achieve your sales targets and goals.
  • Forecast deals appropriately and accurately using Outreach’s forecast methodology.
  • Develop and deliver on account plans, using MEDDPICC Sales Methodology, to strategically increase new revenue for Outreach.
  • Conduct effective discovery calls to identify and unlock business challenges that Outreach solves.
  • Demonstrate how the Outreach platform provides a tailored solution to a prospect’s key outcomes they are trying to achieve.
  • Partner effectively with internal resources to drive and close business, effectively navigating team members across go-to-market, as well as finance and leadership.
  • Negotiate contracts with cross-functional teams including C-suite, finance, procurement, and technical teams.
  • Understand prospect’s needs to develop accurate scoping and success criteria to position a successful implementation.
  • Provide smooth account transition once landed to professional services and account management teams for future adoption and expansion opportunities.
  • Operate with high integrity while adhering to internal processes and sales methodologies.
  • Perform other duties as assigned.

Basic Qualifications

  • At least eight years of sales lifecycle management experience, preferably in a SaaS environment
  • Proven experience in selling disruptive, complex solutions into medium to large organizations
  • Proven experience in selling into accounts through a top down executive motion
  • Ability to manage and navigate long sales cycles (12 months) with contract values up to $200k - $500k
  • Ability to build and cultivate strong, trusting relationships and partnerships, both internally and externally
  • Knowledge of the MEDDPICC Sales Methodology and experience applying and using this framework for successful sales
  • Strong pipeline management skills
  • Strong negotiation skills
  • Effective communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to tailor messaging appropriately for the audience
  • Executive presence and interpersonal skills
  • Strong business and financial acumen to be able to showcase metrics and potential ROI
  • Team player with a high sense of drive and initiative to keep opportunities moving forward to create a winning culture

Why You’ll Love It Here

  • 25 days holiday + 8 bank holidays
  • Outreach contributes with monthly contribution towards your pension
  • Private medical care for employee and spouse/family with Program Health Plus
  • Cashplan is offered through Medicash to help offset out of pocket medical related expenses
  • Dental coverage
  • Life insurance at 4x annual salary
  • 16 weeks of annual top up maternity leave pay or 8 weeks of fully paid paternity leave
  • A parental leave program that includes not just extended time off but options for a paid night nurse
  • Opportunity to be part of company success via equity program
  • Diversity and inclusion programs that promote employee resource groups like OWN+ (Outreach Women's Network), Adelante (Latinx community), OBX (Outreach Black Connection), Mosaic (AAPI community), Pride (LGBTQIA+), Gender+, Disability Community, and Veterans/Military
  • Employee referral bonuses to encourage the addition of great new people to the team

Outreach is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

Our success is reliant on building teams that include people from different backgrounds and experiences who can elevate assumptions and ideas with fresh perspectives. We're dedicated to hiring the whole human, not just a resume. To that end, we look for a diverse pool of applicants-including those from historically marginalized groups. We would like to invite you to apply even if you don't think you meet all of the requirements listed below. We don't want a few lines in a job description to get between us and the opportunity to meet you.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
reason: The position is geared towards enterprise accounts and managing complex sales cycles, marking it as B2B.
fit_explanation: This job is a strong fit for the candidate due to their extensive experience in enterprise technology sales and their current work in building agentic AI systems for B2B sales pipeline automation, which aligns well with Outreach's focus on leveraging AI for revenue teams. The candidate's proven track record in closing complex deals and scaling partner ecosystems across diverse regions complements the role's requirements for driving new logo prospecting and deal acceleration. Additionally, their expertise in building go-to-market strategies from the ground up will be valuable in enhancing Outreach's sales execution platform.

title: Enterprise Account Executive – AI
companyindustry: Software Development, Technology, Information and Media, and Information Services
descriptionhtml:
Location: London, UK

Salary: Up to £120k base / £240k OTE


Sell into tier-1 banks in a category being rebuilt from the ground up by AI


About the Role


This is high-stakes enterprise selling from day one. You’ll be working with some of the largest financial institutions in Europe, in conversations that are already live and commercially serious.


  • Own a named territory across tier-1 banks, payments firms, and regulated enterprises
  • Run full-cycle enterprise deals with significant ACV and expansion potential
  • Engage senior stakeholders across compliance, risk, technology, and the C-suite
  • Partner directly with founders on major deals and strategic accounts
  • Play a key role in shaping how enterprise sales is built as the company scales
  • Leverage a mix of outbound, founder-led momentum, and strong market demand


About the Company


  • AI platform transforming compliance and onboarding for enterprise financial institutions
  • Recently raised €20M+ from leading global investors
  • Founded by operators with deep experience inside banks and fintech
  • Already working with major brands including Santander, Monta, and Mercuryo
  • Strong enterprise traction with high-value deals and real market pull
  • Lean, high-calibre team scaling across London and Europe


Ideal Candidate Profile


  • 5+ years closing enterprise deals into financial services or regulated fintech
  • Experience selling compliance, data, workflow, or platform products
  • Proven ability to navigate complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise sales cycles
  • Strong credibility with senior buyers in regulated or technical environments
  • Experience operating in early-stage or scaling startup environments
  • Additional European language is a strong advantage

title: Enterprise Sales / Account Executive Microsoft Business Applications & Cloud (m/w/d)
descriptionhtml:
Über uns

Let’s build your future together!

Bei Prodware arbeitest du nicht in starren Konzernstrukturen, sondern in verantwortungsvollen Projektteams, die Unternehmen mit Microsoft‑Technologien nachhaltig transformieren. Als einer der führenden europäischen Microsoft‑Partner verbinden wir über 30 Jahre Beratungserfahrung mit Innovationskraft in ERP, CRM, Data, BI & AI – international aufgestellt, im Team nahbar. Wir glauben an Vertrauen statt Mikromanagement, an Mut zu neuen Lösungen und an Leidenschaft für das, was wir tun.

Gestalte mit uns die digitale Zukunft als

Enterprise Sales / Account Executive Microsoft Business Applications & Cloud (m/w/d)

Wir suchen dich zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt zur Verstärkung eines unserer Teams in Deutschland oder Österreich.

Deine Rolle bei uns:

Du bist Owner für Growth in deinem Portfolio: von der ersten Hypothese bis zum Closing und zur Expansion.

  • Strategic Account Ownership: Du entwickelst Account-Pläne, baust Multi-Stakeholder-Netzwerke (C-Level bis Fachbereich) und führst Deals als End-to-End Owner.
  • Consultative / Value-Based Selling: Du übersetzt Business-Strategien in Roadmaps und Business Cases (ROI/TCO), führst Value Workshops und steuerst Entscheidungen strukturiert.
  • Deal Orchestration: Du leitest komplexe Sales Cycles (inkl. RFP/RFI, Procurement, Legal, Security/Compliance), koordinierst PreSales/Delivery/Partner und hältst Forecast & Next Steps sauber.
  • Microsoft Co-Sell & Ecosystem: Du nutzt Microsoft-Kanäle aktiv (Co-Sell, Field Alignment, Partner-Ökosystem), um Pipeline und Closing-Probability zu steigern.
  • Land & Expand: Du schließt initiale Transformationsumfänge ab und entwickelst gezielt Attach/Expansion (Power Platform, Data/AI, Security, Managed Services/Support).
  • Exzellenz im Handwerk: Du arbeitest mit klaren Sales-Routinen (MEDDICC/ähnlich), disziplinierter CRM-Hygiene, starker Storyline und messbarer Pipeline-Steuerung.


Was du mitbringst:

Wir suchen keine Buzzwords, sondern verlässliche Performance in komplexen B2B-Deals.

  • Proven Track Record: Mehrjährige Erfahrung im B2B Solution Selling (ERP/CRM/Cloud/Data/AI/ Security) mit nachweisbaren Abschlüssen im Mittelstand/Enterprise-Segment.
  • Enterprise Sales Skills: Du beherrschst Multi-Stakeholder Selling, Buying-Center-Management, Discovery, Qualification (z. B. MEDDICC), Verhandlungsführung und Deal Governance.
  • Business & Tech Credibility: Du kannst mit CFO/COO/IT-Leads auf Augenhöhe sprechen und komplexe Microsoft-Lösungen verständlich in Business Outcomes übersetzen.
  • Struktur & Drive: Du arbeitest konsequent mit Account-Plänen, Mutual Action Plans, sauberem Forecasting und hoher Selbstorganisation.
  • Value Storytelling: Du kannst Nutzenargumente klar formulieren (Time-to-Value, Risk Reduction, Compliance, Scalability) und überzeugend präsentieren.
  • Ecosystem Mindset: Du arbeitest partnerschaftlich mit Microsoft, ISVs und internen Teams – und nutzt das aktiv zur Pipeline-Beschleunigung.
  • Language & Presence: Sehr gutes Deutsch und gutes Englisch (C-Level tauglich), professionelles Auftreten, starke Moderations- und Workshop-Fähigkeiten.
  • Optional Plus (kein Muss): Erfahrung mit Dynamics 365 (F&O/CE/BC), Power Platform, Azure Data/AI oder Managed Services; Erfahrung mit RFPs/öffentlichen Ausschreibungen.


Warum Prodware:

  • Gestaltungsfreiheit statt starrer Prozesse – wir vertrauen auf verantwortungsbewusste Menschen
  • Zusammenarbeit auf Augenhöhe in interdisziplinären Teams
  • Flexible Arbeitsmodelle und Raum für eigenverantwortliches Arbeiten
  • Gezielte Weiterentwicklung durch Trainings, Zertifizierungen und Projektpraxis
  • Ein attraktives Vergütungs‑ und Benefit‑Paket, u. a.:

description_html:
Über uns

Let’s build your future together!

Bei Prodware arbeitest du nicht in starren Konzernstrukturen, sondern in verantwortungsvollen Projektteams, die Unternehmen mit Microsoft‑Technologien nachhaltig transformieren. Als einer der führenden europäischen Microsoft‑Partner verbinden wir über 30 Jahre Beratungserfahrung mit Innovationskraft in ERP, CRM, Data, BI & AI – international aufgestellt, im Team nahbar. Wir glauben an Vertrauen statt Mikromanagement, an Mut zu neuen Lösungen und an Leidenschaft für das, was wir tun.

Gestalte mit uns die digitale Zukunft als

Enterprise Sales / Account Executive Microsoft Business Applications & Cloud (m/w/d)

Wir suchen dich zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt zur Verstärkung eines unserer Teams in Deutschland oder Österreich.

Deine Rolle bei uns:

Du bist Owner für Growth in deinem Portfolio: von der ersten Hypothese bis zum Closing und zur Expansion.

  • Strategic Account Ownership: Du entwickelst Account-Pläne, baust Multi-Stakeholder-Netzwerke (C-Level bis Fachbereich) und führst Deals als End-to-End Owner.
  • Consultative / Value-Based Selling: Du übersetzt Business-Strategien in Roadmaps und Business Cases (ROI/TCO), führst Value Workshops und steuerst Entscheidungen strukturiert.
  • Deal Orchestration: Du leitest komplexe Sales Cycles (inkl. RFP/RFI, Procurement, Legal, Security/Compliance), koordinierst PreSales/Delivery/Partner und hältst Forecast & Next Steps sauber.
  • Microsoft Co-Sell & Ecosystem: Du nutzt Microsoft-Kanäle aktiv (Co-Sell, Field Alignment, Partner-Ökosystem), um Pipeline und Closing-Probability zu steigern.
  • Land & Expand: Du schließt initiale Transformationsumfänge ab und entwickelst gezielt Attach/Expansion (Power Platform, Data/AI, Security, Managed Services/Support).
  • Exzellenz im Handwerk: Du arbeitest mit klaren Sales-Routinen (MEDDICC/ähnlich), disziplinierter CRM-Hygiene, starker Storyline und messbarer Pipeline-Steuerung.


Was du mitbringst:

Wir suchen keine Buzzwords, sondern verlässliche Performance in komplexen B2B-Deals.

  • Proven Track Record: Mehrjährige Erfahrung im B2B Solution Selling (ERP/CRM/Cloud/Data/AI/ Security) mit nachweisbaren Abschlüssen im Mittelstand/Enterprise-Segment.
  • Enterprise Sales Skills: Du beherrschst Multi-Stakeholder Selling, Buying-Center-Management, Discovery, Qualification (z. B. MEDDICC), Verhandlungsführung und Deal Governance.
  • Business & Tech Credibility: Du kannst mit CFO/COO/IT-Leads auf Augenhöhe sprechen und komplexe Microsoft-Lösungen verständlich in Business Outcomes übersetzen.
  • Struktur & Drive: Du arbeitest konsequent mit Account-Plänen, Mutual Action Plans, sauberem Forecasting und hoher Selbstorganisation.
  • Value Storytelling: Du kannst Nutzenargumente klar formulieren (Time-to-Value, Risk Reduction, Compliance, Scalability) und überzeugend präsentieren.
  • Ecosystem Mindset: Du arbeitest partnerschaftlich mit Microsoft, ISVs und internen Teams – und nutzt das aktiv zur Pipeline-Beschleunigung.
  • Language & Presence: Sehr gutes Deutsch und gutes Englisch (C-Level tauglich), professionelles Auftreten, starke Moderations- und Workshop-Fähigkeiten.
  • Optional Plus (kein Muss): Erfahrung mit Dynamics 365 (F&O/CE/BC), Power Platform, Azure Data/AI oder Managed Services; Erfahrung mit RFPs/öffentlichen Ausschreibungen.


Warum Prodware:

  • Gestaltungsfreiheit statt starrer Prozesse – wir vertrauen auf verantwortungsbewusste Menschen
  • Zusammenarbeit auf Augenhöhe in interdisziplinären Teams
  • Flexible Arbeitsmodelle und Raum für eigenverantwortliches Arbeiten
  • Gezielte Weiterentwicklung durch Trainings, Zertifizierungen und Projektpraxis
  • Ein attraktives Vergütungs‑ und Benefit‑Paket, u. a.:
    • 30 Tage Urlaub & b ezahlte Freistellung an Heiligabend & Silvester
    • Betriebliche Altersvorsorge & Gesundheitsmanagement

Additionally, the candidate's background in building go-to-market strategies and scaling partner ecosystems complements Prodware's emphasis on innovation and collaboration in transforming businesses with Microsoft technologies. title: Sales Executive (CA6F430)
companyindustry: Financial Services, Capital Markets, and Data Infrastructure and Analytics
descriptionhtml:
Referment is partnering with a fast growing FinTech scale-up that provides advanced data and analytics solutions to global financial institutions. As the company continues to expand across the UK and EMEA, they are looking to hire a highly driven Sales Manager to focus on new logo acquisition and drive growth across capital markets clients.

This is a true hunter role suited to someone who thrives on building pipeline, opening doors with senior stakeholders, and closing complex SaaS or data platform deals within competitive markets.

The Role

This is a hands-on, individual contributor role where you will take ownership of new business development across buy-side and sell-side institutions as well as other financial market participants. Working closely with senior leadership, marketing and product teams, you will identify new opportunities, develop targeted prospecting strategies, and lead complex enterprise sales cycles from initial engagement through to closing. This role requires a high degree of autonomy and is suited to a self-starter who can be effective immediately without requiring significant support or training to be successful.

Key Responsibilities Include

  • Driving new logo acquisition across financial institutions in the UK and EMEA
  • Developing targeted outbound strategies to build and convert high quality pipeline
  • Engaging C-level executives and senior decision makers with a consultative sales approach
  • Managing full sales cycles from prospecting and qualification through negotiation and close
  • Executing direct sales strategies to win new business independently of partnership channels
  • Representing the firm at industry events, client meetings and conferences
  • Collaborating with product and marketing teams to refine messaging and respond to evolving market needs
  • Providing market feedback to influence product strategy and go-to-market initiatives
  • Typical deal sizes range from £50k to £150k with sales cycles of 3 to 12 months.

What They Are Looking For

An essential requirement for this role is a proven track record as a new business hunter, with demonstrable success in exceeding sales targets through new logo acquisition rather than account management. You must have experience selling complex SaaS, data, or technology solutions directly into Capital Markets firms (e.g., Investment Banks, Asset Managers, Hedge Funds).

  • Experience working for a market data or financial technology provider such as Bloomberg, Moody's, Refinitiv, Factset, Exegy, or Virtu Financial is highly valued.
  • Experience operating in high-growth scale-up environments is essential for this role. We are looking for candidates who thrive in agile settings rather than those coming from large corporate brands or professional services firms.
  • A stable career history is essential. We are looking for candidates who have demonstrated longevity and commitment, typically with 2+ years in each of their most recent roles and no more than three employers over the last seven years.
  • This is a hands-on, individual contributor role focused on personal sales execution and direct new business acquisition; this position does not carry team management or strategic leadership responsibilities and is not suited for those who have previously held "Head of" or "CRO" level roles.
  • We are also open to high-potential SDR-level candidates who can demonstrate strong SaaS platform sales experience and a hunter mindset.
  • Candidates must be able to demonstrate full dedication to this role and the company's growth, with no conflicting external business interests or side ventures.
  • Strong ability to build relationships with senior stakeholders across buy-side or sell-side organisations.
  • Confidence managing complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise sales cycles from inception to close with minimal supervision and a high degree of autonomy.
  • Strong interest in emerging technologies including AI and machine learning in financial services.
  • Excellent written communication skills and attention to detail.

The Opportunity

  • Join a rapidly scaling FinTech business expanding across global markets
  • High impact role focused purely on new business growth
  • Competitive base salary of £50k to £90k depending on experience and seniority (with the lower end of the range reserved for high-potential SDR-level talent)
  • 100% OTE with strong earning potential
  • Hybrid working with a requirement to be in the London office three days per week
  • Opportunity to sell innovative data and analytics solutions used by leading financial institutions

If you are a driven FinTech sales professional who enjoys opening new doors and closing complex deals, please apply.

#Referment

description_html:
Referment is partnering with a fast growing FinTech scale-up that provides advanced data and analytics solutions to global financial institutions. As the company continues to expand across the UK and EMEA, they are looking to hire a highly driven Sales Manager to focus on new logo acquisition and drive growth across capital markets clients.

This is a true hunter role suited to someone who thrives on building pipeline, opening doors with senior stakeholders, and closing complex SaaS or data platform deals within competitive markets.

The Role

This is a hands-on, individual contributor role where you will take ownership of new business development across buy-side and sell-side institutions as well as other financial market participants. Working closely with senior leadership, marketing and product teams, you will identify new opportunities, develop targeted prospecting strategies, and lead complex enterprise sales cycles from initial engagement through to closing. This role requires a high degree of autonomy and is suited to a self-starter who can be effective immediately without requiring significant support or training to be successful.

Key Responsibilities Include

  • Driving new logo acquisition across financial institutions in the UK and EMEA
  • Developing targeted outbound strategies to build and convert high quality pipeline
  • Engaging C-level executives and senior decision makers with a consultative sales approach
  • Managing full sales cycles from prospecting and qualification through negotiation and close
  • Executing direct sales strategies to win new business independently of partnership channels
  • Representing the firm at industry events, client meetings and conferences
  • Collaborating with product and marketing teams to refine messaging and respond to evolving market needs
  • Providing market feedback to influence product strategy and go-to-market initiatives
  • Typical deal sizes range from £50k to £150k with sales cycles of 3 to 12 months.

What They Are Looking For

An essential requirement for this role is a proven track record as a new business hunter, with demonstrable success in exceeding sales targets through new logo acquisition rather than account management. You must have experience selling complex SaaS, data, or technology solutions directly into Capital Markets firms (e.g., Investment Banks, Asset Managers, Hedge Funds).

  • Experience working for a market data or financial technology provider such as Bloomberg, Moody's, Refinitiv, Factset, Exegy, or Virtu Financial is highly valued.
  • Experience operating in high-growth scale-up environments is essential for this role. We are looking for candidates who thrive in agile settings rather than those coming from large corporate brands or professional services firms.
  • A stable career history is essential. We are looking for candidates who have demonstrated longevity and commitment, typically with 2+ years in each of their most recent roles and no more than three employers over the last seven years.
  • This is a hands-on, individual contributor role focused on personal sales execution and direct new business acquisition; this position does not carry team management or strategic leadership responsibilities and is not suited for those who have previously held "Head of" or "CRO" level roles.
  • We are also open to high-potential SDR-level candidates who can demonstrate strong SaaS platform sales experience and a hunter mindset.
  • Candidates must be able to demonstrate full dedication to this role and the company's growth, with no conflicting external business interests or side ventures.
  • Strong ability to build relationships with senior stakeholders across buy-side or sell-side organisations.
  • Confidence managing complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise sales cycles from inception to close with minimal supervision and a high degree of autonomy.
  • Strong interest in emerging technologies including AI and machine learning in financial services.
  • Excellent written communication skills and attention to detail.

The Opportunity

  • Join a rapidly scaling FinTech business expanding across global markets
  • High impact role focused purely on new business growth
  • Competitive base salary of £50k to £90k depending on experience and seniority (with the lower end of the range reserved for high-potential SDR-level talent)
  • 100% OTE with strong earning potential
  • Hybrid working with a requirement to be in the London office three days per week
  • Opportunity to sell innovative data and analytics solutions used by leading financial institutions

If you are a driven FinTech sales professional who enjoys opening new doors and closing complex deals, please apply.

#Referment
reason: The role involves complex enterprise sales cycles and focuses on new business acquisition in a FinTech environment.
fit_explanation: This job is a good fit for the candidate due to their extensive experience in enterprise technology sales, particularly in closing complex deals and building go-to-market strategies. Their background in data analytics and SaaS aligns well with the requirements of the Sales Executive role at Referment, especially given the focus on acquiring new clients in the financial sector. However, the candidate's experience is primarily in B2B sales and may need to be supplemented with specific knowledge of capital markets to fully excel in this position.

title: Enterprise Account Executive - UK
descriptionhtml:
About Us

Acceldata is the market leader in Enterprise Data Observability. Founded in 2018 and backed by top investors including Insight Partners, March Capital, Lightspeed, Sorenson Ventures, Industry Ventures, and Emergent Ventures, we are a Series-C funded company headquartered in Silicon Valley.

Our Enterprise Data Observability Platform—the first of its kind—helps enterprises build and operate world-class data products by ensuring data is reliable, trusted, and ready to power today’s most critical technologies, including AI, LLMs, Analytics, and DataOps.

Delivered as a SaaS solution, Acceldata is trusted by leading global organizations such as HPE, HSBC, Visa, Freddie Mac, Manulife, Workday, Oracle, PubMatic, PhonePe (Walmart), Hershey’s, Dun & Bradstreet, and many more.

About The Role

We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Account Executive to join our growing team. As an Account Executive, you will be a crucial member of our sales force, responsible for cultivating and managing relationships with our valued clients. Your primary focus will be on driving revenue growth, expanding our customer base, and delivering exceptional customer experiences.

Responsibilities

Sales Strategy and Planning:

  • Develop and execute strategic sales plans to achieve and exceed revenue targets within assigned territory.
  • Identify target accounts and build strong relationships with key decision-makers throughout the organization.
  • Maintain strong sales pipeline and forecast to increase visibility of short and long-term prospects through Salesforce

Client Acquisition And Expansion

  • Prospect, qualify, and close new enterprise accounts within the Fortune 500 while expanding relationships within the existing customer base.
  • Build and maintain relationships with account decision-makers and decision influencers, key business partners, and influencers in the territory
  • Comprehensively understand client needs and pain points, positioning our software solutions as essential tools for their business success.

Product And Industry Expertise And Demonstration

  • Maintain a deep understanding of our software products and effectively communicate their value propositions to potential clients.
  • Conduct product demonstrations and presentations that highlight the benefits of our solutions in addressing client challenges.
  • Monitor industry competitors, new products, and market conditions to understand a customer's specific needs

Collaboration With Cross-Functional Teams

  • Work closely with marketing, product development, and customer success teams to ensure a cohesive and integrated approach to client engagement.
  • Provide feedback from the field to help shape product development and marketing strategies.
  • Manage and grow partner relationships at the field level with ISVs and GSIs to drive revenue and client success

Contract Negotiation And Closing

  • Lead negotiations and contract discussions, addressing client concerns and objections effectively.
  • Close deals in a timely manner while ensuring customer satisfaction and long-term success.

Requirements

  • 10+ years of quota-carrying, enterprise software sales into the Fortune 500 with a proven track record of exceeding quota.
  • Strong Experience prospecting, driving, orchestrating, and closing complex sales cycles within the Data Quality, Governance, Orchestration, Catalog, Management, or Reliability industries.
  • Experience using Salesforce and other sales tools (Clari, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Outreach, ZoomInfo, etc) to automate tactical elements of the sales process and accelerate deal velocity and increase productivity.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to articulate complex, value oriented and architecture concepts clearly and concisely.
  • Willingness to develop territory via outbound prospecting, the SDR team, marketing, and partner organization.
  • Excellent technical skills and understanding of databases, data warehousing, Cloud, ETL, and related eco system technologies.
  • Comfortable speaking with data engineers, Chief Data Officers, and others with deep domain expertise in enterprise data management.
  • Ability and willingness to work in a fast-paced and dynamic team environment.
  • Ability to travel up to 50% meeting with prospects, visiting customers, trade shows, and other customer facing events.
  • Willingness to work outside of work hours when necessary to meet client facing customer requirements (meeting presentation, contract negotiations, and other critical “move the needle” events.

At Acceldata, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of job history, disability, gender identity, religion, race, color, caste, marital/parental status, veteran status, or any other special status. We stand against the discrimination of employees and individuals and are proud to be an equitable workplace that welcomes individuals from all walks of life if they fit the designated roles and responsibilities.

is all about working with some of the best minds in the industry and experiencing a culture that values an ‘out-of-the-box’ mindset. If you want to push boundaries, learn continuously, and grow to be the best version of yourself, Acceldata is the place to be!

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

descriptionhtml:
Are you passionate about hunting for new business and driving growth within the UKI enterprise market? 

Kahoot! is looking for a Senior Account Executive, New Business to join our North Sales team (UKI, Nordic & Benelux) with a specific focus on the UKI region. 

Join Kahoot!, one of the world's fastest-growing SaaS companies, and be part of our dynamic growth journey! In this role, you won't just sell software; you will act as a trusted advisor to new Enterprise customers (+2,000 employees), identifying opportunities to solve business challenges and ensuring our solutions deliver high-value impact within their organizations.You will be the architect of our future growth, spearheading activities to acquire new business through a structured, value-based approach. If you thrive in a global environment, embrace the challenge of the "hunt," and are driven to succeed, this is an exceptional opportunity to excel within our EMEA Sales team.

This position is based in our office centrally located in Copenhagen, Denmark or Oslo, Norway.

The role

Your main responsibility will be to strategically expand your portfolio of new clients across targeted industries in UKI. You will be a key member of the team, responsible for the entire sales cycle from initial prospecting to closing deals with some of the world's leading companies.In this role, you will report to the Regional Sales Manager.



Responsibilities

  • Proactively build and grow a strong pipeline of large-account (2,000+ employees) opportunities through targeted new logo acquisition and driving expansion opportunity within your Book of Business (UKI based companies)
  • Lead a solution and value-based sales process from initial prospecting and pipeline building to closing
  • Be the subject matter expert in presenting the value and drive the sales process across the Kahoot! suite
  • Work closely with BDR, Solution Engineering, Customer Success and Channel teams to tailor solutions and ensure exceptional customer experiences throughout the sales process
  • Effectively manage and communicate with senior key stakeholders to align Kahoot!'s solutions with their core business goals
  • Maintain a high standard of pipeline coverage (4x+ coverage ratio), forecasting rigor and CRM hygiene: weekly commit/best‑case, risk/upsides, crisp close plans, and maintaining an accurate hygiene
  • Consistently exceed quarterly revenue targets through disciplined execution
  • Measure success through disciplined account execution, enterprise deal velocity, deal quality (average deal size, conversion rates), sales cycle management, and forecast accuracy


Requirements

  • BA/BS degree or equivalent
  • Proven enterprise SaaS track record, preferable from the UK market, with ≥100% quota attainment and multiple multi‑threaded deals
  • End‑to‑end ownership of 3-9 month expansion deal cycles, navigating Procurement, Legal, and InfoSec, with disciplined use of MEDDICC to control the sales process
  • Proven success in managing and growing a Book of Business, demonstrated success in generating and closing new enterprise logos and securing expansions
  • Rigid forecasting rigor and CRM hygiene (weekly commit/best‑case, risks/upsides, accurate product mix) with hands‑on. proficiency, preferable in HubSpot
  • Strong business acumen and ability to navigate large organisations, engaging decision-makers and key stakeholders
  • Comfortable in a result-oriented environment where things are moving fast
  • A positive, proactive attitude and a growth-oriented minds
  • Can relate to our Commercial Mantras: Connecting, Driving, Impacting
  • Fluent in English, both verbal and written.

At Kahoot!, we know that the best ideas come from working closely together. That's why we're working from the office four days a week—collaborating, sharing ideas, and building meaningful relationships while working on our exciting and ambitious goals. If you thrive in a dynamic, creative environment where teamwork and strong connections are at the heart of everything we do, this is the place for you.

Benefits

  • A competitive compensation package
  • Paid maternity and paternity leave
  • Home broadband allowance
  • Mobile subscription
  • Lunch contribution
  • Flexible working
  • Buddy program 
  • Social and company events (virtual and in person)
  • A diverse, friendly and international environment

About Kahoot!

The Kahoot! Group is on a mission to make learning awesome! We want to empower everyone, including children, students, and employees to unlock their full learning potential. Our Kahoot! learning platform makes it easy for any individual or corporation to create, share, and host learning sessions that drive compelling engagement.

Launched in 2013, Kahoot!'s vision is to build the leading learning platform in the world. Since launch, Kahoot! has hosted hundreds of millions of learning sessions with more than 10 billion participants (non-unique) in more than 200 countries and regions. The Kahoot! Group includes Clever, the leading US K-12 EdTech learning platform, together with the learning apps DragonBox, Poio, Drops, Actimo, Motimate, and Whiteboard.fi.

The Kahoot! Group is headquartered in Oslo, Norway, with offices in the US, the UK, France, Finland, Estonia, Denmark, Spain and Poland. To learn more, visit us at kahoot.com, and let's play!

Our K!rew

At the Kahoot! Group we champion a positive culture of collaborative learning and innovation. With a global team of more than 500 employees representing over 50 different nationalities, we're a diverse and fun bunch of people! We work hard and celebrate our wins, tackle challenges with original ideas and learn something new every day. Every day we inspire our customers into new opportunities, leveraging our platforms and driving world-class employee engagement, learning and communication. We are driven by our mission and guided by our values: playful, curious and inclusive.  

Does this sound like the right right for you? Join us in making learning awesome!
